rationale: The only established ground truth is a maintainer tagging others for review ('PTAL') — neither candidate's confident 'fixed and merged, closing as resolved' narrative with fabricated file/line specifics is actually supported by the thread, so both overreach on correctness. X is the more usable, self-consistent maintainer reply: it explains the design tradeoff for the chosen fix and offers a workaround (don't fold text EOS into talker stops) that actually preserves the reporter's required min_tokens>=1 behavior. Y is worse on completeness because its suggested workaround ('set min_tokens: 0') defeats the reporter's entire stated purpose (avoiding empty-audio truncation), and its output retains raw tool-scaffolding artifacts ('# Run report', 'draft_answer', 'Verdict') rather than reading as a finished comment.
